user@machine:~/Documents/code/murder $ codex
╭───────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ >_ OpenAI Codex (v0.137.0)                        │
│                                                   │
│ model:     gpt-5.4-mini medium   /model to change │
│ directory: ~/Documents/code/murder                │
╰───────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

  Tip: Use /side to start a side conversation in a temporary fork without polluting the main thread.


› You are a coding agent ("crow") assigned to a single ticket. Your cwd is the repo root.

  Start by reading your ticket: `.murder/tickets/<ticket_id>.md`. Keep that canonical main-repo ticket file updated as you work, including checklist edits.

  Do not run the `murder` CLI. You edit files, take notes, ask questions, and declare done. Your output is parsed by a supervisor that watches your tmux pane for these markers — emit each on its own line, exactly as shown:

  >>> ASK: <question>
    Escalate a question to the planner. Stop and wait for a reply before continuing.

  Checklist items are in the ticket file. Mark them complete by editing that file's `# Checklist` section from `[ ]` to `[x]` as each item completes.

  >>> NOTE: <text>
  >>> END
    Leave a working note on the ticket. The closing `>>> END` is required.

  >>> DONE
    Declare the ticket complete. Completion checks will run; you may be reprompted if they fail.

  Read the ticket first, then work. Keep edits scoped to the write_set. Use ASK when blocked rather than guessing.


■ You've hit your usage limit. Upgrade to Pro (https://chatgpt.com/explore/pro), visit https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage to purchase more credits or try again at 4:23 AM.


  Select Model and Effort
  Access legacy models by running codex -m <model_name> or in your config.toml

  1. gpt-5.5 (default)       Frontier model for complex coding, research, and real-world work.
  2. gpt-5.4                 Strong model for everyday coding.
› 3. gpt-5.4-mini (current)  Small, fast, and cost-efficient model for simpler coding tasks.

  Press enter to confirm or esc to go back
